author: @make425
text: Good thing you ain't going fast. You really need support on them outer wheel bolts. Built many peaces and parts rides. You don't need a wheel bolt breaking or a weld . Please 🙏 safety first.

author: @Earzone63
text: WHAT?!! The thought of sticking a Rowing machine on a kids bike and actually doing it! This is beginning to cross into mad inventor territory! :DD (Reminds me of Furze)

author: @danielgreenaway1587
text: Rob, I am interested in purchasing a dynamo pedalo mechanism for my life raft. Would power 600w wheelchair motor at full tilt. How much do you require for the pedals and Genny mate?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: sorry mate - I don't sell things like that

author: @pgprentice
text: welding certainly is easier than bolting stuff together.  Have you got sunburned from welding yet?  Steering and rowing is going to be a dexterous challenge :)

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: nope not yet mate - I guess I don't do enough of it

author: @captainprototype187
text: I need to start welling and cutting too! I just found a hoverboard.  The 36v batpack seems to be faulty but the motors are the wheels .  I saw about 30 neo magnets inside each one. Thinking about clearing out my cellar.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: that is an awesome find - you have to do something with that for sure- I only started welding about 6 months ago and I had never welded before - it opens up a whole world of possibilities!

author: @lightcapmath2777
text: Lovely! you ROCK! DVD:)

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: Thanks! You too!

author: @ryanlebeck259
text: Use a hand switch for the drive and the petals for steering, that way it's a matter of grip on the handles and not dual foot motions. Easier to operate mate.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: cheers mate - don't forget though - it's really a rowing machine on wheels so both hands and feet are used for drive

replies.author: @ryanlebeck259
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering a fantastic idea all round. Keep inspiring 😁

author: @MrTubeuser12
text: LOL, I can see these two doing a Muppet-cosplay as the mad professor and poor old beaker getting the raw end of the experiment, LOL  "MeMeMe",  " it's ok beaker, you don't need breaks"

replies.text: lol

author: @scotttovey
text:
Row, Row, Row your bike
gently down the walk.
Merrily, Merrily, Merrily, Merrily;
watch the people gawk.

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: should turn a few heads i would think lol

author: @iancrosby3475
text: Used to use cycling projects' row bike a few years ago that they had for disability cycling.  You couldn't fall off it and you could operate it with no legs, which was awesome but it operated like a boat, with a lever  on each side like an oar operating two sets of pulleys.  You couldn't see where you were heading and If you didn't coordinate both sides you went off track, but steering was technically possible

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: i have seen wheelchairs done like that

replies.author: @iancrosby3475
replies.text: @ThinkingandTinkering  I forget who made the row bike, but it was pretty cool.  It may have been a wheelchair manufacturer, like Da Vinci.  Loads of fun to ride, they had all kinds of cycles and did weekly sessions  on running tracks

author: @jimsmindonline
text:
Totally bonkers but I love it! ❤
I hope the steering works out, "You'll get used to it"... I hope! 🤣
Would be interesting to add the flywheel back in as an energy harvesting system, you could spin it up downhill and give yourself a boost back up again! Or row it up to speed and use it to set off with extra acceleration at traffic lights!

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: it is for sure lol - i have added the freewheel back in - cheers

author: @stevengibson9240
text: Old school I love it!

author: @martinbonner8626
text: Going in a straight line may be ok , but given no differential , turning could be more difficult

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: it'll probably be like a trike - mostly pedal versions of trikes don't have differentials either - might be a lot to do with it is being pedalled - I don't know but we will find out lol

replies.author: @nil5221
replies.text: If rear tyre scrub is a problem, perhaps move the rear wheels closer together or even change to a single wheel. Or only drive one wheel.

author: @czechyorker
text: "Row, Row, Row Your Bike" is a New English language nursery rhyme by Robert Murray-Smith. ;-)
